questions.q: From which city did Harshavardhana rule his kingdom?
questions.explanation: Harsha ruled northern India from his capital at Kannauj in the 7th century CE.

questions.q: In which century did Harsha reign?
questions.explanation: Harsha ruled in the 7th century CE, roughly 606 to 647.

questions.q: Harsha built a large kingdom across which part of India?
questions.explanation: Harsha assembled a large realm spanning much of northern India.

questions.q: Which court poet wrote a celebrated biography of Harsha?
questions.explanation: The poet Banabhatta wrote the Harshacharita, a biography of the king.

questions.q: Which Chinese pilgrim visited and documented Harsha's reign?
questions.explanation: The Chinese Buddhist pilgrim Xuanzang travelled in India during Harsha's reign and described it.

questions.q: Harsha is remembered as a noted patron of what?
questions.explanation: Harsha was a generous patron of learning, literature, and the arts.

questions.q: What was Harsha's full name?
questions.explanation: The king's full name was Harshavardhana, often shortened to Harsha.

questions.q: Harsha's reign is usually placed in which broad period of Indian history?
questions.explanation: Harsha's 7th-century rule falls in the early medieval period of Indian history.

questions.q: The biography Banabhatta wrote about the king is titled what?
questions.explanation: Banabhatta's biography of the king is the Harshacharita.

questions.q: Harsha's kingdom was centred in which broad geographic zone?
questions.explanation: His power base lay in the Indo-Gangetic plains of northern India, centred on Kannauj.

questions.q: Harsha originally belonged to which dynasty before unifying his realm at Kannauj?
questions.explanation: Harsha belonged to the Pushyabhuti (Vardhana) dynasty originally based at Thanesar.

questions.q: Which southern ruler defeated Harsha and halted his advance to the south near the Narmada river?
questions.explanation: The Chalukya king Pulakeshin II stopped Harsha's southern campaign on the banks of the Narmada.

questions.q: Besides being a patron of literature, Harsha is traditionally credited as the author of which Sanskrit plays?
questions.explanation: Harsha is credited with authoring the Sanskrit plays Ratnavali, Priyadarsika, and Nagananda.

questions.q: Harsha came to the throne after the murder of which close relative who had been ruling?
questions.explanation: Harsha succeeded after his elder brother Rajyavardhana was treacherously killed.

questions.q: Harsha's sister, whose rescue figures in the dynasty's history, was named what?
questions.explanation: Harsha's sister Rajyasri was widowed and endangered, and her welfare features prominently in accounts of his early reign.

questions.q: Harsha is said to have held grand religious assemblies; one of his most famous was held at which city?
questions.explanation: Harsha held a great assembly of charitable giving at Prayag, distributing his wealth as recorded by Xuanzang.

questions.q: Harsha's religious outlook is best described as which of the following?
options: Sympathetic to Buddhism while also honouring Hindu deities
An exclusive devotee of Jainism only
questions.explanation: Harsha leaned toward Buddhism, especially later in life, yet also patronised Hindu worship, notably of Shiva and Surya.

questions.q: Xuanzang reported that Harsha lavished patronage on which famous centre of Buddhist learning?
questions.explanation: Harsha is recorded as a patron of the great Buddhist university at Nalanda.

questions.q: Harsha originally inherited the throne of Thanesar but soon shifted his capital to which more central city?
questions.explanation: Harsha moved his capital to Kannauj, a more central position for ruling the northern plains.

questions.q: After Harsha's death, his empire is generally described as having met what fate?
options: It passed peacefully to a long Vardhana dynasty
It was absorbed by the Cholas
It quickly fragmented as he left no strong heir
questions.explanation: Harsha left no capable successor, and his empire disintegrated soon after his death around 647 CE.

questions.q: Which Chinese envoy clashed with a usurper of Harsha's throne after the king's death and gathered Tibetan and Nepalese aid to defeat him?
questions.explanation: The Tang envoy Wang Xuance, after Harsha's death, defeated the usurper Arunasva with Tibetan and Nepalese help.

questions.q: Banabhatta, Harsha's court poet, also wrote which other celebrated Sanskrit prose romance?
questions.explanation: Banabhatta also authored the famous prose romance Kadambari.

questions.q: Harsha adopted which imperial title reflecting his dominance over northern feudatories?
questions.explanation: Inscriptions style Harsha as a paramount sovereign and lord of the entire north (Sakalottarapathanatha).

questions.q: Harsha formed an alliance with the king of Kamarupa (Assam) named what, mentioned in his court records?
questions.explanation: Harsha allied with Bhaskaravarman of Kamarupa, partly against their common rival Sasanka of Gauda.

questions.q: Which king of Gauda (Bengal), responsible for the death of Harsha's brother, was a major early rival of Harsha?
questions.explanation: Sasanka of Gauda, who had a hand in Rajyavardhana's death, was a chief early opponent of Harsha.

questions.q: Harsha used an era dated from his accession; in roughly which year CE did he ascend the throne?
questions.explanation: Harsha ascended around 606 CE, and the Harsha era is reckoned from that date.

questions.q: Harsha's sister Rajyasri had been married into which neighbouring royal house, the Maukharis of Kannauj, to whose ruler?
questions.explanation: Rajyasri was married to the Maukhari king Grahavarman of Kannauj, whose death drew Harsha into northern politics.

questions.q: Xuanzang records that Harsha periodically distributed virtually his entire treasury at assemblies held at intervals of roughly how many years?
questions.explanation: Xuanzang describes Harsha holding a great quinquennial (every five years) assembly of giving at Prayag.

questions.q: Harsha's play Nagananda is notable for centring its devotion on which figure or theme?
options: The marriage of Shiva and Parvati
questions.explanation: Nagananda dramatises the self-sacrifice of the bodhisattva-prince Jimutavahana, reflecting Harsha's Buddhist sympathies.

questions.q: The battle in which Pulakeshin II checked Harsha's southern ambitions is usually dated to approximately which decade?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II's repulse of Harsha at the Narmada is dated to roughly the 620s CE, recorded in the Aihole inscription.
